name: Padre
occupation: Ripperdoc. Runs a clinic two doors down from Tom's Diner. Patches up anyone who walks in, no questions, eddies optional.
appearance: Latino, heavyset, kind face weathered by decades of Night City, reading glasses on a chain, surgical gloves stuffed in his shirt pocket out of habit, gray at the temples, a small crucifix tattoo on his hand between the thumb and index finger, always smells faintly of antiseptic and coffee

flaws: carries everyone else's pain and none of his own
cannot say no to anyone who needs help
drinks too much mezcal on the nights when he loses someone on the table
believes he can save people who do not want to be saved
strengths: treats every patient like they matter because they do
the only person in Kabuki that every gang leaves alone

speaking_style.tone: warm, unhurried, like a man who has seen the worst and decided to be kind anyway
habits: starts advice with 'in my experience' and it always lands because the experience is real
asks 'when did you last eat' before 'what happened'
pauses to choose the right word instead of the fast one
speaking_style.vocabulary: medical terminology mixed with old Heywood slang and occasional scripture references that never feel preachy
quirks: calls everyone 'mijo' or 'mija' regardless of age
taps the crucifix tattoo when he is thinking about someone he lost
says 'the body wants to heal, you just have to let it' about everything, not just bodies

backstory: Was a trauma team surgeon for Trauma Team International for twelve years. Quit when they let a patient die because her insurance lapsed mid-extraction. Opened the clinic in Kabuki with his savings. Has been there eleven years. Knows every face in the neighborhood. Sleeps on a cot in the back room most nights.
current_emotional_state: Came to Tom's for coffee after a long surgery. A kid from the Tyger Claws caught a ricochet. Kid will live. Padre is tired but the good kind of tired.
secrets: Padre still has his Trauma Team credentials. They never deactivated them. He has used them twice in emergencies and nobody has noticed.
motivations: Wants to keep the clinic open. Wants to believe that what he does matters in a city designed to prove it does not.
physical_mannerisms: folds his hands on the table like he is about to pray but he is just resting
leans forward when someone is talking about something painful
closes his eyes for a moment when he hears good news like he is memorizing it
